title: 第 63 節
en: — gifts to priests, 272. Damascius says Brahmans lived at Alexandria saee. V, 62.

Deussen, Professor, theory of evolution of Word and Brahman, Deva, supreme, never asserted by Kapila, 302.
— worshippers of the Devas, 241 Devas, thirty-three in number, according to Rig-veda and Avesta, difficulty of filling up this number, 38.

Devayana, path of the gods, 176. Devotion to the Lord, one of many expedients, 319. Dharma, duty, 199, 440. Dharmakirtti, seventh century, 364, Dharmamegha, cloud of virtue, Dharmarakshita, a sage, 439.
Dhatn, maker, name given to the one god, 47.
— Nyaya-samuMaya, a Tibetan translation of, 365 n.

Dipawkara Sri^nana, 439. Distinction of good and evil, 180. Divakara, a sage, 600 A D., 30. Divine thinker, every word an act of a, 150. Divyadasa Datta, living Yedantist, Dosha, faults, 421. Dreaming, state of, 174. Dnshfam, what is seen, 274. Dr-ish&nta, example, 385.
Drumstick and drum together convey, even to the deaf, the idea of sound, 380.
Dual gods, two or three gods working together, tendency towards unity among the gods, 40.
EFFECT, an, only a new manifestation, dogma characteristic of the Samkhya, 158 Ekagrata, concentration, 357.

— of works, the independent, 331. Exercises, Abhyasa, 338. Exposition, five-mernbered form of, FABLES in the Sutras, 305. Fa-hiaii visits India, 399-414 A. D., Fancy chiefly due to words, 337. Fetishism or Totemism, did they precede the Aryan theogony?
